reasoning: As of April 2, 2026, Nana Dzagnidze is ranked #19 on the FIDE women's list published on Wikipedia. For the first resolution date (April 19), no new FIDE list will have been released, meaning the page will perfectly reflect the April 1 rankings and her rank will remain #19, satisfying the condition. However, for May 2026 and beyond, new lists will be published. At age 39, Dzagnidze is in the twilight of her absolute peak (historically reaching #3 with a 2573 rating, now down to 2469). She sits precariously at #19, with Harika Dronavalli (#20) just 3 points behind, alongside many rapidly climbing younger players like Divya Deshmukh. Over the next 1-12 months, she faces a strong probability of dropping to #20 or lower, pushing our estimates slightly below 0.5. As we forecast further out (2029-2036), typical aging curves strongly suggest she will drop out of the top 20 entirely. However, honoring the constraint to let profound long-term ambiguity reflect in probabilities closer to 0.5 (e.g., changes to Wikipedia's formatting, FIDE system updates, or unexpected career revivals), the probabilities revert slowly back towards a coin toss for the 3-10 year horizons.
